verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I membrane protein with a single transmembrane domain, belonging to the p24 trafficking protein family. NCBI explicitly states localization to both plasma membrane and Golgi cisternae, indicating dual compartmentalization. As a GOLD-domain trafficking protein involved in vesicular transport and secretase complex assembly, it cycles between the ER-Golgi system and the PM as part of its normal function in anterograde and retrograde trafficking. The documented PM pool arises from its role in vesicular transport machinery rather than stable outer-leaflet residence, qualifying as contextual dual localization. No evidence of stable TM-partner tethering or cell-state-induced display.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Proportion and stability of PM pool vs. dominant Golgi/ER residence not precisely characterized; unclear whether PM pool from active trafficking cycling is large/stable enough to prioritize as a therapeutic target, but documented dual compartmentalization confirms accessibility during vesicular cycling.
